methodology: Generated on Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS using local apt-cache metadata plus spot inspection of the corresponding upstream source trees shipped in the Ubuntu archive, including optional or vendored cJSON integrations that can be rebuilt against the packaged library inside the Ubuntu 24.04 harness.

dependents.notes: Ubuntu 24.04 source metadata and freerdp3-dev depend on libcjson-dev, but the published runtime packages such as libfreerdp3-3 and freerdp3-x11 do not directly depend on libcjson1.

runtime_functionality: The library emits sender and receiver transport statistics as JSON objects delivered through the stats callback interface.
The CLI tools inherit that runtime use transitively through librist4.
Parses headset and controller JSON configuration blobs, including Vive and Index device data.
Reads and writes stereo camera calibration JSON files.
Persists GUI calibration scene state as JSON.
The broker's dynamic-security plugin loads and saves its security configuration as JSON.
mosquitto_sub and related client-side JSON output modes render message data and MQTT properties with cJSON.
Parses MusicBrainz JSON responses and cached JSON metadata for CD and release lookups.
Serializes account definitions and loaded-account status information as JSON.
Parses OpenID Connect device-flow JSON responses.
Updates encrypted account configuration files by editing their JSON refresh-token field.
Builds JSON command objects for management and CLI operations such as status, ping, conf get/set, and conf ls.
Parses JSON command output when printing management responses.
Parses JSON request bodies for REST endpoints that inject move, button, touch, and swipe input events.
Saves tracker projects as JSON-based .snibb files and loads them back at runtime.
Parses JSON application configuration for buffer, fullscreen, preview, and color settings.
Parses Eclipse keyword-definition JSON files through Json::JsonObject and the parser keyword loader.
Serializes well and group state back into JSON objects for runtime reporting and data export.
dependents.notes: Ubuntu 24.04 source metadata build-depends on libcjson-dev, and the Debian packaging excludes the vendored external/cjson fallback, so the noble build uses the shared libcjson package.

runtime_functionality: Client and server JSON modes emit structured test-start, interval, and summary objects for throughput runs.
The shared libiperf codebase builds those JSON result trees through helper routines in iperf_util.c.
dependents.notes: Ubuntu 24.04 ships the cJSON sources vendored under src/, and the package does not build-depend on libcjson-dev. The harness swaps that bundled copy out for the packaged safe libcjson to validate JSON-mode compatibility against the system library.

runtime_functionality: The scripting engine exposes JSON_EXPLODE, JSON_IMPLODE, and JSON_ERROR builtins backed by cJSON.
The shipped regress/json script exercises round-trip JSON parsing and serialization through those builtins.
dependents.notes: Ubuntu 24.04 ships cJSON vendored in source/, and the package does not build-depend on libcjson-dev. The harness rebuilds epic5 against the packaged safe libcjson to validate the scripted JSON builtins with the system library.
